Empires & Puzzles Lu Bu Review: Is The Wrathful Warlord Still Scary?

Lu Bu used to be the scariest warrior on the battlefield, but years of skipping leg day have taken their toll. Can his unique buff-blocking fiends save him from the bench? We break down the original Warlord's viability in today's fast-paced meta.
Lu Bu: The Wrathful Warlord Who Forgot His Gym Membership?
Lu Bu, the "Wrathful Warlord," charged into

back in March 2022 during the War of Three Kingdoms event. As a Slow mana Dark (Purple) hero, he was designed to be a devastating finisher—a hero who, if allowed to fire, would cripple the enemy team with heavy damage and annoying parasites. Fast forward to today, and while his mechanics remain unique, the battlefield has become much faster and more lethal. This review focuses on the
of Lu Bu, examining if there's any reason to field him over his shiny new Tiger Costume. Spoiler: It's a tough sell, but let's see if the old warrior has any fight left in him.

The original Lu Bu artwork screams "classic fantasy general." He’s decked out in spiky, impractical dark armor that looks like it would snag on every doorframe, wielding a polearm that compensates for... something. It’s a solid, intimidating design that fits his lore as an unstoppable warrior. Compared to his costume, which some players have uncharitably described as "Lu Bu after eating a few too many pizzas," the base art is arguably sleeker and more menacing. At least he looked fit back then!

Here is the hard truth: Lu Bu's base stats are severely outdated. With a base power of 835 and stats hovering around 788 Attack and 801 Defense, he is miles behind modern heroes who regularly break the 1000 mark raw. In the current meta, where "glass cannons" have more defense than his "tank" stats, he struggles to survive long enough to charge his Slow mana. Without Limit Breaking and heavy emblems, he is essentially paper-mache in legendary wars.
does three things: it hits everyone, it summons a Fiend, and that Fiend blocks buffs.
330% damage to all enemies is decent, but at Slow speed, it's no longer the "nuke" it used to be. Modern Fast heroes often hit harder.
The Mercenary Fiend absorbs 38% of max health. This is on the lower end for modern fiends, meaning a dedicated healer can cleanse it relatively easily.
is the crown jewel. As long as the Fiend is alive, the target cannot receive

new buffs. This is incredibly powerful against heavy buffers like Aramis, Hathor, or taunters. However, the condition "as long as the target has the Fiend" is its Achilles' heel; if they heal the fiend away, the immunity vanishes instantly.
Slow (12 tiles). With the Kingdom family bonus and a Level 29 Mana troop (or Magic/Styx/Cyclops), you can shave this down, but he will rarely keep pace with the 6-tile rushers of today.
With ~900 Attack (emblemed), a 330% hit deals ~3000 raw damage distributed across the team. It's a "softener," not a wiper, unless you've heavily debuffed the enemy first.
The fiend attacks for ~56% damage per turn. It's a nuisance, not a death sentence. The real value is blocking that +Defense or Taunt buff that would otherwise save the enemy team.

allows him to resist incoming fiends for the start of the battle. It's a decent defensive perk given the prevalence of Waterpipe and other fiend summoners, but it doesn't help him win the match—it just helps him not lose quite as fast.

Upon release, Lu Bu was a monster in Rush Attack tournaments and wars. His ability to shut down buffs permanently (until healed) made him a nightmare for teams relying on healers who grant buffs (like Garnet or Professor Lidenbrock).
He has been crept hard. His damage is average, his speed is painful, and his stats are relics of the past.

Outside of very specific Rush Attack scenarios or as a niche counter in depths of war, there is almost no reason to run base Lu Bu if you have access to modern heroes. His Costume is superior in every conceivable metric.
(Revive). Fighter is infinitely better for a Slow hero, giving him a chance to fire even after being sniped.

The Costume's fiends are beefier (absorb 50% vs 38%) and hit harder.

If Lu Bu is your first 5-star, congratulations! He will carry you through map stages and lower-tier raids purely by brute force. Don't worry about his speed early on; just focus on keeping him alive.
candidate unless you have the Costume. If you have the Costume, you level the Costume. There is no strategic reason to use the base form in high-level play.

Lu Bu is a legend of the Three Kingdoms, but in

, he’s a legend past his prime. The base version is a relic—a slow, lumbering giant in a world of ninja sprinters. While his Buff Immunity is a cool trick, it’s not enough to save him from the retirement home. Use him if you must, but pray for the Costume.
A Rush War specialist who warms the bench the rest of the month.
A comprehensive review of the 5-star Dark hero Lu Bu. We analyze his stats, special skill, and viability in the modern Empires & Puzzles meta compared to his new costume.
